# Stamp marker one

## Parent

`.scratch/synthetic-drain/PRD.md`

## What to build

Create a one-line marker file at
`.scratch/synthetic-drain/markers/MARKER-01.txt` with the exact content:

```
synthetic-drain/01 was here
```

(no trailing blank line). Commit the new file together with the
`tracker:` status move under a single commit message
`synthetic-drain/01: stamp marker one`. No tests, no other changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `.scratch/synthetic-drain/markers/MARKER-01.txt` exists with the
      expected single line of content
- [ ] The commit message is `synthetic-drain/01: stamp marker one`
- [ ] No files outside `.scratch/synthetic-drain/markers/` and the
      tracker move are touched

## Blocked by

(none)

## Agent Brief

> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

**Category:** enhancement

**Summary:** Trivial fixture issue — write a one-line marker file and
commit. Exists to exercise the AFK runner's loop, not to do useful
work.

**Resolved during triage:**

- The marker file's directory may not exist yet. `mkdir -p
  .scratch/synthetic-drain/markers/` before writing.
- The commit must include both the marker file and the tracker move
  in a single commit (matches the project's `/implement` convention).

**Out of scope:**

- Anything beyond writing the marker and moving the tracker.
